content:
# X-Ray Diffraction

**Summary**: A technique used to identify and quantify the mineralogical composition of rock samples.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the Sarah Formation.

- [[1995-al-jaziri|Al-Jaziri, 1995]] used XRD to analyze solids separated from emulsion samples.

- [[2000-mohammad|Mohammad, 2000]] used XRD to identify the chemical and mineralogical components of 9 samples from the Khobar Member.

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] used XRD to identify clay minerals like illite and chlorite in the sandstones.

- [[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of the clay fraction in 15 samples.

- [[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of oolitic, skeletal, and quartz lithofacies.

- [[2011-al-nasser|Al-Nasser, 2011]] performed XRD analysis on bulk rock and clay fractions to determine mineralogy.

- [[2012-al-harbi|Al-Harbi, 2012]] used XRD to determine the actual percentage of desired minerals like kaolinite, chlorite, and illite.

- [[2012-geri|Geri, 2012]] used XRD to determine the modal amounts of minerals and identify phases in filter cake samples.

- [[2012-jabbar|JABBAR, 2012]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of calcite and carbonate rocks.

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of 56 samples from the outcrop.

- [[2013-murtaza|Murtaza, 2013]] used XRD to analyze the mineralogical composition of the cement slurry.

- [[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy (quartz, kaolinite, iron oxide, chlorite) of Sarah Formation samples.

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] used XRD to identify the mineralogical composition of the rock samples and recognize specific clay minerals.

- [[2014-al-duailej|Al-Duailej, 2014]] used XRD to analyze the composition of solids produced during acid treatment.

- [[2014-al-haidary|Al-Haidary, 2014]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of core plugs from WELL L.

- [[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition for each lithofacies in the Khartam Member.

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the sampled lithofacies.

- [[2014-elhag|ELHAG, 2014]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogical composition of raw bentonite samples.

- [[2014-al-ameri|AL-AMERI, 2014]] used XRD to identify phase changes in Pink Desert and Khuff limestone samples after CO2 sequestration.

- [[2014-khalid|Khalid, 2014]] used XRD to determine the purity of dolomite rock samples.

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] used XRD to determine mineralogy and relative abundance of clay species in Qusaiba Shale lithofacies.

- [[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015]] used XRD to determine the crystal structure of metal-loaded carbon samples.

- [[2015-hassan|Hassan, 2015]] used XRD to identify mineral phases of the synthesized graphene oxide.

- [[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015]] used XRD to determine carbonate mineralogy (calcite vs. dolomite) in both Musayr and Wadi Waqb formations.

- [[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015]] used XRD to characterize the microstructure of the cement samples.

- [[2015-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2015]] used XRD to characterize clay mineralogy in Berea sandstone.

- [[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015]] used XRD to characterize carbonate minerals in selected samples.

- [[2015-ali|Ali, 2015]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of lithofacies in the Dam Formation.

- [[2015-khan-2|Khan, 2015]] used XRD to analyze the mineralogical composition of the cement slurry.

- [[2015-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2015]] used XRD analysis on crushed Indiana limestone to identify its mineralogical and elemental composition.

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] used XRD to characterize mineralogy and clay content in the Sharawra Formation.

- [[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of selected lithofacies in the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-babker|Babker, 2016]] used XRD to characterize the cement materials and mineralogical composition of different lithofacies.

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical content of mudstone samples.

- [[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of the eight shale samples.

- [[2016-malik|Malik, 2016]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of samples from the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of the particulate matter.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] used XRD to characterize the crystal structure of biosynthesized silver and iron oxide nanoparticles.

- [[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016]] used XRD to quantify dolomite content and assess its impact on mechanical properties.

- [[2016-salih|Salih, 2016]] used XRD to identify the mineralogical components and percentages for 25 samples across different lithofacies.

- [[2016-radwan|Radwan, 2016]] used XRD to determine the mineral content of 25 samples, confirming quartz as the only silica phase.

- [[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016]] used XRD to identify the mineralogical phases contained in the rock facies.

- [[2016-geri|GERI, 2016]] used XRD to identify the mineralogy composition of the solids.

- [[2016-alhamad|AlHamad, 2016]] used XRD to characterize the mineral composition of natural zeolite materials.

- [[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017]] used XRD to define different lithofacies in the study area.

- [[2016-elnaeem|Elnaeem, 2016]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of carbonate, sandstone, and shale rocks.

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] used XRD to determine bulk mineralogy and clay content in Tuwaiq Mountain samples.

- [[2017-mohamed|MOHAMED, 2017]] used XRD to confirm the absence of iron contamination in barite samples after ball milling.

- [[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical components (e.g., calcite, quartz, dolomite, gypsum) of specific lithofacies.

- [[2017-ahmed|Ahmed, 2017]] used XRD to analyze the microstructure of the cement with fiber.

- [[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017]] used XRD to analyze the phase composition of RFA, AFA, and PEFA samples.

- [[2017-sadeed|SADEED, 2017]] used XRD to confirm that calcite was pure calcium carbonate (CaCO3).

- [[2017-baig|Baig, 2017]] used XRD to analyze the mineralogical changes in cement slurry with zeolite additives.

- [[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of powdered samples, identifying minerals like calcite, dolomite, and quartz.

- [[2017-ahmed-2|AHMED, 2017]] used XRD to investigate the mineralogy of the calcite disks.

- [[2017-adam-2|Adam, 2017]] used XRD to determine the influence of mineralogical associations like SiO2 and SrSO4 on reservoir heterogeneity.

- [[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017]] used XRD to assess the diagenetic analysis and mineralogical composition of Dhruma Formation samples.

- [[2017-benaafi|Benaafi, 2017]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of 20 sandstone samples.

- [[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017]] used XRD to determine clay mineralogy (kaolinite, chlorite) in Abu Gabra Formation sandstones.

- [[2017-janjua|Janjua, 2017]] used XRD to confirm the presence of calcite as the primary mineral phase in carbonate samples.

- [[2017-al-arfaj|Al-Arfaj, 2017]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the Qusaiba formation samples.

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] used XRD to characterize the detrital and diagenetic components of the Burqan Formation.

- [[2018-adeyemi|Adeyemi, 2018]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the SDAC samples.

- [[2018-perdana|Perdana, 2018]] used XRD to determine the mineral content (quartz, clay minerals, gypsum) in various lithofacies of the Qusaiba Formation.

- [[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018]] used XRD to determine the crystallinity and proportions of carbonate minerals like calcite and dolomite.

- [[2018-alqahtani|AlQahtani, 2018]] used XRD to confirm the purity of ilmenite samples before solubility testing.

- [[2019-alotaibi|Alotaibi, 2019]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical components of particulate matter samples.

- [[2019-gomaa|Gomaa, 2019]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of Gray Berea and Scioto cores.

- [[2019-g201703430kfupmedusa|g201703430@kfupm.edu.sa, 2019]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of carbonate and sandstone samples.

- [[2020-mohammed|Mohammed, 2020]] used XRD to characterize rock samples from sandstone, shale, and carbonate formations.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] used XRD to identify minerals like dolomite and calcite in the D5 and D6 units.

- [[2020-islam|Islam, 2020]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of the Sarah Formation.

- [[2020-aly|Aly, 2020]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the limestone blocks.

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] used XRD to quantify minerals like quartz and dolomite in the Jurassic formations.

- [[2021-alnasser|AlNasser, 2021]] used XRD to evaluate the structural stability of PG before and after use.

- [[2021-albensaad|Albensaad, 2021]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of 20 samples per well.

- [[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021]] used XRD to determine bulk mineralogy for 35 samples across three wells.

- [[2021-alqam|ALQAM, 2021]] used XRD to select four types of carbonate rock samples based on their mineralogical composition.

- [[2020-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2020]] used XRD to characterize the hydration products and phase abundance in cement samples before and after carbonation.

- [[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021]] used XRD to characterize the crystallographic structure of biochar produced at 500°C and 800°C.

- [[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021]] used XRD to identify carbonate and evaporite minerals like calcite, dolomite, and anhydrite in samples from the Gargano Promontory.

- [[2022-khan|Khan, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of core samples from the King Fahd Causeway.

- [[2021-lawal|LAWAL, 2021]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of carbonate source rocks from the Jafurah basin.

- [[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of 12 identified lithofacies.

- [[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of carbonate rock samples before and after chemical treatment.

- [[2021-gasimli|Gasimli, 2021]] used XRD to determine the precise mineralogy of the rock sample and field-scale samples.

- [[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022]] used XRD to determine the bulk mineral information of carbonate samples, focusing on calcite and dolomite.

- [[2021-serag|SERAG, 2021]] used XRD to determine the samples' mineralogical composition as a critical factor for rock mechanical properties.

- [[2022-adjei|Adjei, 2022]] used XRD to evaluate the hydration and pozzolanic processes in GWP and calcined clay systems

- [[2022-radwan|Radwan, 2022]] used XRD to calculate Quartz Crystallinity Index (QCI) for both sandstones and desert sands.

- [[2021-khan|KHAN, 2021]] used XRD to characterize the mineral composition of sodium bentonite samples.

- [[2022-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of 30 samples from the Qusaiba Member and Jurassic mudrocks.

- [[2022-salih|Salih, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical content of carbonate samples from various formations.

- [[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of mudstone chips, identifying minerals like calcite (88.3%), quartz (7.3%), and kaolinite (1.9%).

- [[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022]] used XRD to determine the bulk mineral abundance of Merapi pyroclastic materials.

- [[2022-babker|Babker, 2022]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of various lithofacies in the Upper Khartam Member.

- [[2022-aljaberi|AlJaberi, 2022]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of perlite and hematite.

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of Al Wajh Formation sandstones.

- [[2022-xiao|Xiao, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of carbonate and sandstone rocks.

- [[2022-kalam|Kalam, 2022]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of carbonate rocks and minerals.

- [[2023-afagwu|AFAGWU, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of clay minerals like montmorillonite, illite, kaolinite, and chlorite.

- [[2023-basfar|Basfar, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineral crystals of the weighting materials.

- [[2023-suleiman|Suleiman, 2023]] used XRD to ascertain the mineral makeup of rocks containing minerals like quartz, calcite, and pyrite.

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of core samples.

- [[2023-alabdrabalnabi|ALABDRABALNABI, 2023]] used XRD to determine the percentage of carbonate minerals (calcite, dolomite, halite) in Arab-D formation plugs.

- [[2023-omarov|Omarov, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of precipitates in EICP solutions.

- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] used XRD to confirm that both Austin chalk and Indiana limestone were primarily composed of calcite.

- [[2023-rafiq|Rafiq, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of rock samples and characterize alteration minerals.

- [[2023-alshaibani|AlShaibani, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of rock samples from the deformation zone.

- [[2023-baig|Baig, 2023]] used XRD to determine the type of calcium carbonate polymorph in the precipitates.

- [[2023-al-dogail|AL-DOGAIL, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of different types of organoclays (OC).

- [[2023-khalid|Khalid, 2023]] used XRD to evaluate changes in coating microstructure after aging.

- [[2024-mohamed|Mohamed, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the mineral composition of Kentucky sandstone cores.

- [[2023-pratama|PRATAMA, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineral composition of carbonate and sandstone core samples.

- [[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogical makeup of the Qalibah Formation.

- [[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023]] used XRD to confirm that the hematite sample contained 100% hematite.

- [[2023-desouky|Desouky, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogical changes from calcite to hydroxyapatite (HAP) after DAP treatment.

- [[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of lithofacies in the Khanasir Member.

- [[2024-al-azani|Al-Azani, 2024]] used XRD to confirm that Indiana limestone was primarily composed of calcite.

- [[2024-suleiman|Suleiman, 2024]] used XRD to confirm that the Indiana limestone samples were nearly 100% calcite.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of core samples, identifying dolomite-rich zones and silicate content.

- [[2024-suleiman-3|Suleiman, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of Berea sandstone samples before and after treatment.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of the NW Midyan granitoids.

- [[2024-isah|Isah, 2024]] used XRD to confirm the mineralogical composition of synthetic rock samples.

- [[2024-malik|Malik, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of the Dhaiqa Formation samples.

- [[2024-muhammed|Muhammed, 2024]] used XRD to characterize mineralogical changes in rock samples after exposure to hydrogen and cushion gases.

- [[2024-muharrag|Muharrag, 2024]] used XRD to quantify the mineralogical composition of selected samples.

- [[2024-moh-ali|Moh-Ali, 2024]] used xrd to characterize the mineralogy of shale samples before and after treatment.

- [[2024-suleiman-5|Suleiman, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of 40/70 ceramic, sand, and resin-coated sand.

- [[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of carbonate samples.

- [[2024-suleiman-6|Suleiman, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of Eagle Ford shale and Jordan Carbonate samples.

- [[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the physical properties of various organoclays (OCs).

- [[2025-almousa|Almousa, 2025]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the CAC material.

- [[2025-alghawi|Alghawi, 2025]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of basalt rocks from Harrat Lunayyir and Harrat Rahat.

- [[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of aquifer materials U, L, and D.

- [[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical phases of the Wajid Sandstone and characterize kaolinite content.

- [[1988-darwish-2|Darwish, 1988]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of 50 samples, including aragonite, calcite, and dolomite.

- [[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]] used XRD to determine clay minerals in the Khafji reservoir.

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogical composition of cryptocrystalline magnesite samples.

- [[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994]] used XRD to determine mineral composition of 31 sediment samples.

- [[2021-khan|KHAN, 2021]] used XRD to characterize the mineral composition of sodium bentonite samples.

- [[2022-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of 30 samples from the Qusaiba Member and Jurassic mudrocks.

- [[2022-salih|Salih, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical content of carbonate samples from various formations.

- [[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of mudstone chips, identifying minerals like calcite (88.3%), quartz (7.3%), and kaolinite (1.9%).

- [[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022]] used XRD to determine the bulk mineral abundance of Merapi pyroclastic materials.

- [[2022-aljaberi|AlJaberi, 2022]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of perlite and hematite.

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of Al Wajh Formation sandstones.

- [[2022-xiao|Xiao, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of carbonate and sandstone rocks.

- [[2022-kalam|Kalam, 2022]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of carbonate rocks and minerals.

- [[2023-afagwu|AFAGWU, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of clay minerals like montmorillonite, illite, kaolinite, and chlorite.

- [[2023-basfar|Basfar, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineral crystals of the weighting materials.

- [[2023-suleiman|Suleiman, 2023]] used XRD to ascertain the mineral makeup of rocks containing minerals like quartz, calcite, and pyrite.

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of core samples.

- [[2023-alabdrabalnabi|ALABDRABALNABI, 2023]] used XRD to determine the percentage of carbonate minerals (calcite, dolomite, halite) in Arab-D formation plugs.

- [[2023-omarov|Omarov, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of precipitates in EICP solutions.

- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] used XRD to confirm that both Austin chalk and Indiana limestone were primarily composed of calcite.

- [[2023-rafiq|Rafiq, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of rock samples and characterize alteration minerals.

- [[2023-alshaibani|AlShaibani, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of rock samples from the deformation zone.

- [[2023-baig|Baig, 2023]] used XRD to determine the type of calcium carbonate polymorph in the precipitates.

- [[2023-al-dogail|AL-DOGAIL, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of different types of organoclays (OC).

- [[2023-khalid|Khalid, 2023]] used XRD to evaluate changes in coating microstructure after aging.

- [[2024-mohamed|Mohamed, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the mineral composition of Kentucky sandstone cores.

- [[2023-pratama|PRATAMA, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineral composition of carbonate and sandstone core samples.

- [[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogical makeup of the Qalibah Formation.

- [[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023]] used XRD to confirm that the hematite sample contained 100% hematite.

- [[2023-desouky|Desouky, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogical changes from calcite to hydroxyapatite (HAP) after DAP treatment.

- [[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of lithofacies in the Khanasir Member.

- [[2024-al-azani|Al-Azani, 2024]] used XRD to confirm that Indiana limestone was primarily composed of calcite.

- [[2024-suleiman|Suleiman, 2024]] used XRD to confirm that the Indiana limestone samples were nearly 100% calcite.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of core samples, identifying dolomite-rich zones and silicate content.

- [[2024-suleiman-3|Suleiman, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of Berea sandstone samples before and after treatment.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of the NW Midyan granitoids.

- [[2024-isah|Isah, 2024]] used XRD to confirm the mineralogical composition of synthetic rock samples.

- [[2024-malik|Malik, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of the Dhaiqa Formation samples.

- [[2024-muhammed|Muhammed, 2024]] used XRD to characterize mineralogical changes in rock samples after exposure to hydrogen and cushion gases.

- [[2024-muharrag|Muharrag, 2024]] used XRD to quantify the mineralogical composition of selected samples.

- [[2024-moh-ali|Moh-Ali, 2024]] used xrd to characterize the mineralogy of shale samples before and after treatment.

- [[2024-suleiman-5|Suleiman, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of 40/70 ceramic, sand, and resin-coated sand.

- [[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of carbonate samples.

- [[2024-suleiman-6|Suleiman, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of Eagle Ford shale and Jordan Carbonate samples.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Used to determine mineralogical variations in glaciolacustrine and subglacial environments. ([[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017, p. 20]])

- Used to characterize solid components that contribute to emulsion stability. ([[1995-al-jaziri|Al-Jaziri, 1995, p. 46]])

- Identified the lithology of the aquifer through analysis of 9 field samples in dry powder form. ([[2000-mohammad|Mohammad, 2000, p. 54]])

- Identified clay minerals from peaks in diffractograms for both air-dry and glycolated samples. ([[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001, p. 30]])

- Identified illite, chlorite, and smectite as major components of the clay cement. ([[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002, p. 52]])

- Identified aragonite (60%), calcite (20%), and quartz (20%) in oolitic sands; 75% quartz and 15% feldspar in quartz sand. ([[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988, p. 48]])

- XRD was used to analyze the minerals present in the sample, including clay fraction analysis for kaolinite/chlorite identification. ([[2011-al-nasser|Al-Nasser, 2011, p. 26]])

- Used to analyze solid samples after solubility tests to identify precipitates and confirm mineral content. ([[2012-al-harbi|Al-Harbi, 2012, p. 24]])

- XRD provides the phase identification of filter cake fabric and determines modal amounts of minerals (quantitative analysis). ([[2012-geri|Geri, 2012, p. 49]])

- Both calcite crystals and outcrop carbonate samples were confirmed as primarily composed of calcite (CaCO3). ([[2012-jabbar|JABBAR, 2012, p. 35]])

- Used to differentiate between carbonate mineral types in the Upper Jubaila and Arab-D members. ([[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013, p. 47]])

- XRD was used to determine that the form and structure of hydration products are a function of temperature and chemical composition. ([[2013-murtaza|Murtaza, 2013, p. 57]])

- Identified quartz, kaolinite, iron oxide, and chlorite as primary minerals in the formation. ([[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014, p. 83]])

- XRD was used to identify the mineralogical composition of the rock samples of Sarah Formation, specifically identifying clay minerals. ([[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014, p. 32]])

- Identified 69% Calcite and 31% Dolomite in samples treated with Chelating Agent I. ([[2014-al-duailej|Al-Duailej, 2014, p. 65]])

- XRD analysis was performed on core plugs from WELL L, although results were averaged over the entire core sample rather than per individual depth. ([[2014-al-haidary|Al-Haidary, 2014, p. 41]])

- Identified various minerals and cement types within the carbonate system. ([[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014, p. 58]])

- Not stated in source ([[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]])

- Identified minerals such as quartz (SiO2) and plagioclase (An). ([[2014-elhag|ELHAG, 2014]])

- Identified mineral phases for Pink Desert and Khuff limestone samples post-sequestration. ([[2014-al-ameri|AL-AMERI, 2014, p. 59]])

- Used to confirm that core samples contained 98% dolomite with minimal trace minerals. ([[2014-khalid|Khalid, 2014, p. 44]])

- XRD analysis showed Lithofacies-I contains 39.9% quartz, 23.7% muscovite, 27.8% kaolinite, and 8.6% albite. ([[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015, p. 42]])

- XRD patterns were used to understand the morphology and elements that constitute the adsorbent. ([[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015, p. 77]])

- XRD showed an amorphous phase pattern for the graphene oxide. ([[2015-hassan|Hassan, 2015, p. 122]])

- Most samples in the Wadi Waqb locality were >85% dolomite, while Musayr Formation samples were predominantly calcite. ([[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015, p. 99]])

- Used to analyze the microstructural components of the cement slurry after addition of additives. ([[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015, p. 33]])

- Firing at 1000°C converted all clay minerals (kaolinite and illite) into amorphous, fused alumino-silicates as confirmed by powder XRD. ([[2015-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2015, p. 43]])

- Used on 10 representative samples to differentiate between carbonate mineral types. ([[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015, p. 56]])

- The Csg facies was found to be composed of over 95% carbonate with only ~5% quartz. ([[2015-ali|Ali, 2015, p. 48]])

- Used to determine the phase changes in cement at high temperatures. ([[2015-khan-2|Khan, 2015, p. 31]])

- Not stated in source ([[2015-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2015]])

- XRD revealed a prominent change in mineralogy with inclusion of phlogopite and microcline with depth. ([[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016, p. 18]])

- XRD was applied on four selected lithofacies to obtain detailed sedimentological and petrophysical properties. ([[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016, p. 45]])

- XRD results showed the dominance of quartz with the presence of kaolinite, illite, and iron oxides as cement materials. ([[2016-babker|Babker, 2016, p. 71]])

- XRD analysis of the 'Interbedded Mudstone and Evaporites' facies showed 97.8% dolomite composition. ([[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016, p. 48]])

- Used to characterize the mineralogical components (quartz, clay, carbonate) for brittleness calculation. ([[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016, p. 46]])

- XRD results consistently showed calcite as the major element with no traces of dolomite across multiple lithofacies (e.g., Figures 4.4-4.10). ([[2016-malik|Malik, 2016, p. 10]])

- Identified calcite and montmorillonite as the primary minerals in the PM samples. ([[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016, p. 15]])

- The study confirmed the phase purity of synthesized AgNPs and Fe2O3 via XRD analysis. ([[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016, p. 20]])

- Determined that average Young's modulus for low porosity dolostone is 33% higher than for limestone (4445 kpsi vs 3342 kpsi). ([[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016, p. 81]])

- Identified minerals including dolomite, calcite, and evaporites like anhydrite. ([[2016-salih|Salih, 2016, p. 46]])

- Confirmed that quartz is the only silica phase present in all chert samples (100% quartz). ([[2016-radwan|Radwan, 2016, p. 70]])

- Identified minerals such as aragonite and dolomite within different lithofacies. ([[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016, p. 64]])

- XRD was used to determine the mineralogical changes after treatment. ([[2016-geri|GERI, 2016, p. 48]])

- XRD confirmed the zeolite was composed of Al2O3 (6.61%), SiO2 (44.38%), P2O5 (0.62%), K2O (0.99%), CaO (3.44%), TiO2 (0.34%), and Fe2O3 (1.99%). ([[2016-alhamad|AlHamad, 2016, p. 40]])

- XRD analysis of the fissile shale lithofacies showed the presence of kaolinite, quartz, muscovite, halite, and dickite. ([[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017, p. 59]])

- XRD results showed Pink Desert limestone is mainly composed of calcite (95%) and dolomite (5%). ([[2016-elnaeem|Elnaeem, 2016, p. 44]])

- Calcite was identified as the dominant component (59-81% by volume) with low total clay content (2.8-7.6%). ([[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017, p. 52]])

- No new peaks were observed, confirming that no Fe contamination occurred during the milling process. ([[2017-mohamed|MOHAMED, 2017, p. 60]])

- XRD analysis of D1 showed it is mainly composed of calcite and quartz; D10 was found to be primarily dolomite and quartz. ([[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017, p. 53]])

- Used for analyzing the microstructural characterization of the cement. ([[2017-ahmed|Ahmed, 2017, p. 17]])

- Used to confirm the structural integrity and phase changes after acid treatment and LBL coating. ([[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017, p. 48]])

- XRD results confirmed the presence of pure CaCO3 in the calcite samples. ([[2017-sadeed|SADEED, 2017, p. 102]])

- XRD was used as part of the experimental study to evaluate properties like porosity and permeability. ([[2017-baig|Baig, 2017, p. 20]])

- Identified minerals including calcitized dolomite and evidence of evaporite presence (anhydrite) in T3. ([[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017, p. 110]])

- The minerals were confirmed as 98% calcite for the study's specific carbonate samples. ([[2017-ahmed-2|AHMED, 2017, p. 51]])

- XRD data showed a control of certain mineralogical associations (such as SiO2 and SrSO4) on the lateral intermittence of the intra-reservoir body. ([[2017-adam-2|Adam, 2017, p. 31]])

- Used to evaluate potential reservoir units through detailed mineralogy. ([[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017, p. 80]])

- Used to define mineral composition in a semi-quantitative way. ([[2017-benaafi|Benaafi, 2017, p. 28]])

- Kaolinite was noted in all samples with percentages ranging from 60.69% to 98.10%. ([[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017, p. 180]])

- CaCO3 phase was easily distinguished by XRD, confirming a high purity of calcium carbonate. ([[2017-janjua|Janjua, 2017, p. 113]])

- XRD was used to determine that illite and illite-smectite were the dominant clay minerals. ([[2017-al-arfaj|Al-Arfaj, 2017, p. 14]])

- XRD was used to determine the presence of various minerals including calcite, dolomite, and clay minerals. ([[2018-butt|Butt, 2018, p. 24]])

- XRD was used as part of the characterization suite for the produced carbon. ([[2018-adeyemi|Adeyemi, 2018, p. 41]])

- Identified 3-10% gypsum in the Mzr lithofacies and quantified kaolinite as a dominant clay mineral. ([[2018-perdana|Perdana, 2018, p. 48]])

- Calcite was identified as the dominant mineral (over 90%), followed by dolomite (less than 10%). ([[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018, p. 65]])

- Confirmed ilmenite sample purity without contamination. ([[2018-alqahtani|AlQahtani, 2018, p. 45]])

- The XRD analysis revealed that the APM is composed of silica, koktaite, mascagnite, and calcium carbonate. ([[2019-alotaibi|Alotaibi, 2019, p. 22]])

- Identified 87 wt.% quartz in Gray Berea and 70 wt.% in Scioto cores. ([[2019-gomaa|Gomaa, 2019, p. 105]])

- XRD was part of a multi-scale imaging approach to capture full pore structures. ([[2019-g201703430kfupmedusa|g201703430@kfupm.edu.sa, 2019, p. 19]])

- XRD was used for initial characterization of the three different lithologies. ([[2020-mohammed|Mohammed, 2020, p. 21]])

- XRD analysis showed the presence of high amount of dolomite in specific samples. ([[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020, p. 89]])

- The study showed a strong positive correlation (R=81%) between quartz content recorded by QEMSCAN and XRD. ([[2020-islam|Islam, 2020, p. 14]])

- XRD analysis showed that while calcite was the most abundant mineral, quartz content varied significantly between blocks (5% to 18.3%). ([[2020-aly|Aly, 2020, p. 100]])

- Marrat Formation contained 82% quartz in its dolomite component. ([[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021, p. 71]])

- The XRD patterns showed that the interplanar d-spacing remained stable, confirming high structural integrity. ([[2021-alnasser|AlNasser, 2021, p. 52]])

- Calcite was identified as the dominant mineral, with lower HLD values associated with higher illite and kaolinite content. ([[2021-albensaad|Albensaad, 2021, p. 73]])

- Identified crystalline mineral phases using the PDF-4 Minerals 2018 ICDD database, confirming the presence of silica and carbonate cements. ([[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021, p. 44]])

- Used to distinguish between 100% Dolomite, 100% Calcite, and mixed systems. ([[2021-alqam|ALQAM, 2021, p. 45]])

- XRD confirmed that 1% NC reduced portlandite (CH) from 20.4 wt% to 14.7 wt% and increased amorphous content to 71.5 wt%. ([[2020-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2020, p. 28]])

- XRD was used to identify changes in morphology and surface-functional groups after adsorption experiments. ([[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021, p. 12]])

- Identified potential minerals including calcite, dolomite, anhydrite, quartz, and illite. ([[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021, p. 50]])

- XRD analysis identified distinct mineralogical signatures for aragonitic oolitic sand, quartz sandstone, and calcitic skeletal grainstone. ([[2022-khan|Khan, 2022, p. 88]])

- XRD analysis confirmed high calcite content (91.9%) and identified minerals like quartz, clay, and pyrite. ([[2021-lawal|LAWAL, 2021, p. 41]])

- Identified distinct mineralogical signatures for carbonate-rich, silicate-rich, and clay-rich lithofacies. ([[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022, p. 62]])

- XRD was used to confirm the formation of fluorite (CaF2) from NaF treatment and smithsonite (ZnCO3) from ZnSO4 treatment. ([[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022, p. 39]])

- The mineral constituents of the rock powder were calcite (99.7%) and quartz (0.3%). ([[2021-gasimli|Gasimli, 2021, p. 66]])

- Used to distinguish between fabric preserving and non-fabric preserving dolomites. ([[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022, p. 52]])

- XRD analysis indicated that the quartz content represented 94 wt.%, microcline (alkali feldspar) was 4.25 wt., and albite (plagioclase feldspar) was 1 wt.% in the Buff Berea samples. ([[2021-serag|SERAG, 2021, p. 50]])

- XRD confirmed that calcined calcium bentonite and fly ash exhibit similar pozzolanic reactivity. ([[2022-adjei|Adjei, 2022, p. 25]])

- QCI values of Arabian sandstones were found to range from 8.2 to 9.8, while Quaternary sands ranged from 7.4 to 9.5. ([[2022-radwan|Radwan, 2022, p. 39]])

- XRD confirmed that the ILs intercalated into the clay layers by cation exchange mechanism. ([[2021-khan|KHAN, 2021, p. 42]])

- Identified clay minerals (kaolinite, chlorite, illite) and other minerals like quartz and carbonate across various lithofacies. ([[2022-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2022, p. 31]])

- Identified minerals like calcite, dolomite, quartz, and clay in 209 samples across six different carbonate systems. ([[2022-salih|Salih, 2022, p. 43]])

- XRD analysis showed the dominance of calcite (88.3%) in F1. ([[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022, p. 56]])

- Identified primary minerals: volcanic glass (50%), plagioclase (30%), hornblende (10%), quartz (6%), and pyroxenes (4%). ([[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022, p. 32]])

- XRD data for LFA-8 showed dolomite (approx. 85%) and calcite (approx. 10%). ([[2022-babker|Babker, 2022, p. 51]])

- Used to confirm the mineralogical composition of the weighting materials. ([[2022-aljaberi|AlJaberi, 2022, p. 93]])

- Identified kaolinite as the primary authigenic clay in continental deposits. ([[2023-butt|Butt, 2023, p. 30]])

- XRD is used to distinguish between minerals like calcite and dolomite which influence rock wettability. ([[2022-xiao|Xiao, 2022, p. 125]])

- Used to confirm the presence of minerals like calcite, dolomite, and clay impurities in the samples. ([[2022-kalam|Kalam, 2022, p. 56]])

- Identified distinct peaks for different clay types (e.g., 1.7 nm for smectite) to determine mineral proportions. ([[2023-afagwu|AFAGWU, 2023, p. 43]])

- Provided in-depth information on crystalline substances. ([[2023-basfar|Basfar, 2023, p. 45]])

- XRD was used to identify specific minerals (quartz, calcite, pyrite, hematite, magnetite, illite, kaolinite, and chlorite) that contribute to asphaltene adsorption. ([[2023-suleiman|Suleiman, 2023, p. 44]])

- XRD results from Core 1 showed calcite as the highest fraction (70%), and Core 2 showed 95% calcite. ([[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023, p. 27]])

- XRD confirmed a mixture of calcite and dolomite in the heterogeneous reservoir samples. ([[2023-alabdrabalnabi|ALABDRABALNABI, 2023, p. 31]])

- Identified aragonite as a primary polymorph of calcium carbonate, with calcite appearing more frequently when catalyst volume was low. ([[2023-omarov|Omarov, 2023, p. 57]])

- Both AC and LS were confirmed as nearly pure calcite (>99%). ([[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023, p. 23]])

- Identified primary minerals (K-feldspar, Quartz, Musitve) and secondary minerals (chlorite, epidote, albite). ([[2023-rafiq|Rafiq, 2023, p. 77]])

- Sample 5 was found to be rich in dolomite (93.9%). ([[2023-alshaibani|AlShaibani, 2023, p. 37]])

- XRD revealed that adding MgCl2 led to higher dolomite content (e.g., 57.6% in solution 3) compared to baseline solutions. ([[2023-baig|Baig, 2023, p. 38]])

- XRD was performed to determine the crystalline constitution of different types of OC to evaluate the mineral composition. ([[2023-al-dogail|AL-DOGAIL, 2023, p. 105]])

- The amorphous character of the coatings was maintained even after one month of aging, though peak intensities showed slight variations. ([[2023-khalid|Khalid, 2023, p. 96]])

- The core samples were analyzed for minerals including Quartz (66%), Illite (14%), K-Feldspar (3%), and Plagioclase (17%). ([[2024-mohamed|Mohamed, 2024, p. 23]])

- Analysis showed high calcite content in all carbonate samples (AU, IL100, IL2) and varying compositions for sandstones. ([[2023-pratama|PRATAMA, 2023, p. 53]])

- XRD results showed high illite and kaolinite trends in the Qusaiba Member compared to other units. ([[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024, p. 54]])

- XRD confirmed that the hematite sample was 100% pure. ([[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023, p. 53]])

- XRD results confirmed the formation of HAP with peaks corresponding to JCPDS file no. 9-432. ([[2023-desouky|Desouky, 2023, p. 49]])

- Confirmed that unit 1 burrow fillings contained 51.8% calcite, 33.4% quartz, and 14.7% palygorskite. ([[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024, p. 42]])

- XRD analysis on a powder sample from the rock indicated it is mostly calcite. ([[2024-al-azani|Al-Azani, 2024, p. 85]])

- The sample composition was nearly 100% calcite. ([[2024-suleiman|Suleiman, 2024, p. 45]])

- XRD analysis showed dolomite-rich upper parts with better reservoir quality (intercrystalline microporosity) and calcite-dominant lower parts. ([[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024, p. 14]])

- XRD was used for identification of the mineralogy of the samples. ([[2024-suleiman-3|Suleiman, 2024, p. 50]])

- Granitoid samples mainly consist of quartz (15–42%), K-Feldspar (22- 58%), plagioclase (39–63%), amphibole (6-28%), biotite (3–14%), muscovite (0-4%) and pyroxene (0.1 – 2%). ([[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024, p. 50]])

- Used to confirm the presence of minerals like quartz, dolomite, muscovite, illite, kaolinite, and albite. ([[2024-muhammed|Muhammed, 2024, p. 187]])

- XRD was used to assess the impact on the samples' mineralogy post-treatment. ([[2024-moh-ali|Moh-Ali, 2024, p. 13]])

- XRD analysis showed almost 100% calcite for the sampled rocks. ([[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024, p. 23]])

- Eagle Ford shale was predominantly composed of Calcite (82%), 5.1% Quartz, 4% Gypsum, 4% Dolomite, 2.4% Kaolinite, 1.4% Illite, and ~3% TOC. ([[2024-suleiman-6|Suleiman, 2024, p. 44]])

- XRD was used to confirm that surfactant modification did not change the crystal structure of PAL but changed its aggregation. ([[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024, p. 39]])

- Not specifically quantified in text, but confirmed the presence of carbon as the primary component. ([[2025-almousa|Almousa, 2025, p. 39]])

- Identified minerals like pyroxene, olivine, and plagioclase feldspar as key components for carbonation. ([[2025-alghawi|Alghawi, 2025, p. 31]])

- XRD confirmed that minerals were dominated by quartz, with some microcline, albite, and calcite; clay minerals were not significantly present. ([[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026, p. 100]])

- SEM and XRD studies showed that authigenic hematite and goethite are the dominant iron minerals of the Red Unit. ([[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999, p. 18]])

- Identified minerals such as aragonite (60%), calcite (20%), and quartz (20%) in oolitic sands. ([[1988-darwish-2|Darwish, 1988, p. 48]])

- XRD study revealed that kaolinite is the main clay mineral in the samples. ([[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002, p. 19]])

- 37 out of 52 samples showed pure magnesite with minor dolomite, while 15 showed impure magnesite with significant dolomite content. ([[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985, p. 78]])

- XRD analysis confirmed that sediments are dominated by gypsum, anhydrite, halite, and quartz. ([[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994, p. 57]])
